channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 11 new books in April offer a chance to step inside someone else's world
item.description: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.content:encoded: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer

item.title: A humorist faces life with Stage 4 lung cancer: 'The future disappeared for me'
item.description: In 2020, Annabelle Gurwitch went to urgent care for a COVID-19 test and learned she had cancer. She writes about life as a "cancer slacker" in her memoir, The End of My Life is Killing Me.
item.content:encoded: In 2020, Annabelle Gurwitch went to urgent care for a COVID-19 test and learned she had cancer. She writes about life as a "cancer slacker" in her memoir, The End of My Life is Killing Me.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Netflix does Nordic noir right in 'Jo Nesbø's Detective' series
item.description: A tortured Oslo police detective may be on the trail of a psycho killer in this genuinely suspenseful screen adaptation of Jo Nesbø's The Devil's Star.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

A tortured Oslo police detective may be on the trail of a psycho killer in this genuinely suspenseful screen adaptation of Jo Nesbø's The Devil's Star.

(Image credit: Ronald Plante)
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Spring TV is stacked: The shows you won't want to miss right now
item.description: From dystopian drama to Silicon Valley satire, a wave of buzzy new series — and a few big finales — arrives all at once.
item.content:encoded:
The Testaments, a spinoff of The Handmaid's Tale.'/>

From dystopian drama to Silicon Valley satire, a wave of buzzy new series — and a few big finales — arrives all at once.

(Image credit: Patrick Wymore/HBO; HBO Max; Steve Wilkie/Disney)
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans

item.title: Now cancer-free, 'Roastmaster' comic Jeff Ross still finds humor in loss
item.description: Known for his ruthless celebrity roasts, Ross turns inward in his Netflix special, Take a Banana for the Ride, which details the loss of his parents and grandfather.
item.content:encoded: Known for his ruthless celebrity roasts, Ross turns inward in his Netflix special, Take a Banana for the Ride, which details the loss of his parents and grandfather.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: 'The Testaments' star Chase Infiniti discusses her 'rocket ship' career success
item.description: Rising star Chase Infiniti has had a breakout year in Hollywood. She speaks with A Martínez about her upcoming role in the Handmaid's Tale sequel, The Testaments.
item.content:encoded:
Rising star Chase Infiniti has had a breakout year in Hollywood. She speaks with A Martínez about her upcoming role in the Handmaid's Tale sequel, The Testaments.

(Image credit: Aurore Marechal/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: How a stranger encouraged a father struggling with his daughter's health issues
item.description: A man who was trying to support his daughter with an undiagnosed disorder received praise from a stranger. He said it changed their life.
item.content:encoded: A man who was trying to support his daughter with an undiagnosed disorder received praise from a stranger. He said it changed their life.

item.title: New online game 'Five Nights at Epstein's' has parents and schools concerned
item.description: A video game has captured kids' attentions. It's called Five Nights at Epstein's — and the goal is to survive multiple nights on Jeffrey Epstein's island unscathed.
item.content:encoded: A video game has captured kids' attentions. It's called Five Nights at Epstein's — and the goal is to survive multiple nights on Jeffrey Epstein's island unscathed.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Even when Arsenio Hall's show was a hit, 'everyone wanted it to be something else'
item.description: Hall's late-night show gave hip-hop a home on TV and helped propel Bill Clinton to the White House. "I wanted to do this show that didn't exist when I was a kid," he says. Hall's memoir is Arsenio.
item.content:encoded:
Hall's late-night show gave hip-hop a home on TV and helped propel Bill Clinton to the White House. "I wanted to do this show that didn't exist when I was a kid," he says. Hall's memoir is Arsenio.

(Image credit: Kevin Winter)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
item.description: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.
item.content:encoded:
The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

(Image credit: Hector Retamal)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby
